cline_patterns.description: Low-stakes read-only tool calls (readFile, listFiles, searchFiles, listCodeDefinitionNames) are grouped into a single collapsible block rather than shown as individual rows. The group shows a natural-language summary like 'Cline read 4 files, 2 folders' and renders as a compact clickable list. Active (in-progress) tools show TypewriterText animations like 'Reading src/main.rs...' inline, merged with completed items. Files can be clicked to open in the editor. The grouping deduplicates between active and completed items by file path.
cline_patterns.ava_recommendation: Implement a ToolGroupRenderer for read-only tools in AVA desktop. Currently each file read/grep appears as a separate row. Group them into a collapsible summary: 'Read N files, searched M patterns'. Show active reads with animated text, completed reads as clickable file links. This would dramatically reduce visual noise for exploration-heavy tasks.

cline_patterns.description: File edits render an inline diff block directly in the chat. Supports two formats: SEARCH/REPLACE blocks and a new Begin/End Patch format. The block shows a file header with action icon (FilePlus for Add, FileX for Delete, FileText for Update), the file path as a clickable link that opens in the editor, +N/-N addition/deletion counts, and a scrollable diff body with line numbers, gutter indicators (4px colored left border), and background tinting. The block auto-scrolls during streaming with user-scroll-to-override detection. The diff body has a max-height of 320px with expand handle. Multiple file hunks in one patch render as separate collapsible blocks.
cline_patterns.ava_recommendation: AVA desktop needs an inline diff block for the write/edit tools. The key patterns: (1) file header with open-in-editor button, (2) +N/-N stats in the header, (3) left gutter bar (4px border-l) colored green/red instead of full background for add/delete lines, (4) line number column with streaming-aware scroll-follow that stops on manual scroll, (5) max-height with expand handle. The streaming detection (isStreaming flag tracking incomplete patch markers) is important so the block updates live as the edit streams in.

cline_patterns.description: A persistent sticky header at the top of the task shows a todo-list progress bar. Displays 'X/N' count badge + current task text truncated with ellipsis. A thin progress bar animates along the bottom of the header. Click to expand and see the full checklist. Clicking the expanded view opens the TODO file in the editor. Uses a LRU cache (100 entries) for parsed TodoInfo to avoid re-parsing identical text on every render. The progress bar hides at 0% and 100% to avoid showing empty/full states.
cline_patterns.ava_recommendation: AVA's plan system (Plannotator-style) should surface a progress indicator in the desktop chat header during plan execution. Show current step N/total with a progress bar, truncated step text, expand-to-see-all. Currently the plan is a separate message in the chat — making it a sticky persistent header would be more useful during long runs.

cline_patterns.description: Clicking a user message makes it editable inline. On edit, two buttons appear: 'Restore Chat' (checkpoint to just before this message and re-send) and 'Restore All' (checkpoint files + chat + re-send). Enter sends with Restore Chat, Cmd+Enter sends with Restore All. Escape cancels. The text area auto-selects all content when entering edit mode. The @mentions in the original message are highlighted even in read-only view.
cline_patterns.ava_recommendation: AVA desktop should allow clicking user messages to edit and re-send with checkpoint restoration. The two-tier restore (chat-only vs chat+workspace) maps to AVA's existing checkpoint system. This is one of the most impactful UX features — users can fork conversation history from any point.

cline_patterns.description: A progress bar in the task header shows current token usage vs max context window, with exact counts on both ends (e.g., '14.2K ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 200K'). Hover opens a popover with a detailed breakdown: tokens in, tokens out, cache writes, cache reads, total, percentage used. A 'Compact' button appears inline which prompts a confirmation dialog before triggering /compact. The progress bar uses a debounced hover with click-outside dismissal.
cline_patterns.ava_recommendation: AVA TUI already shows tokens/context in the status bar. For the desktop app, a visual progress bar with a hover breakdown would be much clearer. The inline Compact button with confirmation is better than a slash command for discoverability. The breakdown showing cache writes/reads is particularly relevant since AVA uses Anthropic prompt caching.

cline_patterns.feature: Command Output Row with Log File Detection
cline_patterns.description: Terminal command output is rendered with: (1) a status badge with animated pulse dot (green+pulse=Running, yellow=Pending, gray=Completed), (2) the command shown at 60% opacity in a code block, (3) output truncated to 75px (5 lines) with expand handle for more. Auto-scrolls to bottom of output during streaming. Detects log file paths in output ('📋 Output is being logged to: /path/file.log') and renders them as clickable links to open in editor. Output lines escape special chars (tabs, backspace, vertical tabs).
cline_patterns.ava_recommendation: AVA's terminal tool output should adopt the status dot + animated pulse for running commands. The log file path detection is a nice touch — when AVA runs long commands that redirect to a log, auto-detecting and linking the log file would be useful. The 5-line auto-truncation threshold matches common expectations.

cline_patterns.feature: Thinking Row with Gradient Scroll Indicators
cline_patterns.description: The thinking/reasoning display is a collapsible section with a shimmer animation on the title text while streaming ('animate-shimmer bg-linear-90 from-foreground to-description'). When expanded, the content area shows gradient fade-out overlays at the top and bottom when scrollable content exists above or below the viewport. The expanded area has a 200px max-height with hidden scrollbar. Auto-scrolls to bottom during streaming. The title doubles as the toggle button (no separate chevron needed).
cline_patterns.ava_recommendation: AVA's ThinkingRow could add the gradient scroll-shadow indicators to hint at scrollable content. The shimmer effect on the title text during streaming is a polished touch. Currently AVA's thinking display is functional but visually plain.

cline_patterns.description: Messages use react-virtuoso (virtual list) for rendering, so only visible messages are in the DOM. The MessageRenderer uses a factory function createMessageRenderer to create Virtuoso itemContent callbacks while keeping rendering logic encapsulated. Messages are keyed by timestamp. Height change callbacks are used to trigger re-layout. This allows thousands of messages without DOM bloat.
cline_patterns.ava_recommendation: AVA desktop chat list should be virtualized for long sessions. Currently all messages render in DOM. The Virtuoso pattern with factory-function itemContent is clean and worth adopting.

cline_patterns.description: ChatTextArea has a slash command menu that activates on '/' input. The menu shows matching commands from a registry. Also has @ mention support for file/folder/git context with a ContextMenu. The @ mention picker searches live as you type. Selected mentions become inline tokens in the text that visually show the file/folder name. The textarea uses react-textarea-autosize for dynamic height.
cline_patterns.ava_recommendation: AVA TUI already has slash commands. For the desktop chat input, an @ mention system for files/folders would be valuable. Currently users drag-drop files or use file picker. The inline mention tokens in the composer are more composable.

cline_patterns.description: A special grouped message type for browser use tool sessions — renders screenshots, page state, and browser actions as a distinct visual group. This is specific to Cline's browser automation feature.
cline_patterns.ava_recommendation: Not directly applicable unless AVA adds browser tool support.

cline_patterns.description: A persistent bar below the input showing which tool categories are auto-approved (read files, write files, execute commands, browser actions). Each toggle has an icon + label. A per-category granularity modal lets users approve specific file patterns, command patterns, etc.
cline_patterns.ava_recommendation: AVA's permission level is binary (Standard/AutoApprove). Cline's per-category granularity is worth considering. A compact icon bar showing what is auto-approved in the current session would be useful without requiring full settings navigation.

continue_patterns.description: Every code block in AI responses has an inline toolbar with: Apply button (play icon, shows when not yet applied), Accept (checkmark) + Reject (X) buttons (shown after Apply while diff is 'done'), Streaming spinner (shown while applying). The Apply action streams the diff into the file and shows a live diff count. Accept/Reject have keyboard shortcuts (Cmd+Shift+Enter / Cmd+Shift+Backspace). The toolbar also has Copy and Run in Terminal buttons. The file info shows file icon (language-aware), filename, and optional line range.
continue_patterns.ava_recommendation: AVA desktop code blocks should have Apply/Accept/Reject inline toolbar. This is the most distinctive Continue UX pattern. The flow: AI suggests code -> user clicks Apply -> diff streams into file -> user sees diff in editor -> Accept/Reject to commit or revert. AVA's edit tool already does this but without the in-chat Apply button — users must wait for AVA to auto-apply. Adding an explicit Apply flow for code blocks would give users more control.

continue_patterns.feature: ThinkingBlockPeek with Timer and Token Count
continue_patterns.description: The thinking block shows as a pill-shaped button: 'Thinking...' (with AnimatedEllipsis) while in progress, then 'Thought for 2.3s (1847 tokens)' when complete. Clicking expands the full thinking content in a smooth max-h transition. Handles redacted thinking blocks with placeholder text. Tracks start time when inProgress=true, calculates elapsed on completion. The button has rounded-full border-[0.5px] styling making it look like a chip/badge.
continue_patterns.ava_recommendation: AVA should show thinking duration and token count after reasoning completes. Currently thinking shows content but not timing. The 'Thought for Xs' pattern is immediately legible and helps users understand model behavior. The chip/pill button shape is more elegant than the current expanded block approach.

continue_patterns.description: When conversation is compacted, a horizontal rule with 'Previous Conversation Compacted' label is inserted at the compaction point. Messages before the compaction point are rendered at 35% opacity (opacity-35 class), making them visually de-emphasized. The compaction point itself is at full opacity to mark the boundary. Response actions (compact, copy, delete) are dimmed and non-interactive while streaming.
continue_patterns.ava_recommendation: AVA's /compact command produces a summary but doesn't visually mark the compaction point in the chat history. The 'Previous Conversation Compacted' divider + pre-compaction dimming is excellent — users clearly see what context the model actually has. The 35% opacity for old messages is a strong visual signal without hiding content.

continue_patterns.description: Each completed AI response has an action bar that appears below the message (not on hover). Actions: Compact (context-aware color — turns warning yellow when context > 80% or isPruned), Generate Rule (creates a .continuerules file from the response), Continue Generation (when response is detected as truncated), Delete message, Copy. The compact button shows a label text when context is >= 60% full ('Compact conversation' text alongside the icon).
continue_patterns.ava_recommendation: AVA should add a response action bar. The key actions: copy, delete, compact. The context-aware coloring on Compact (warn when context > 80%) is a practical nudge. The Generate Rule feature (creating project rules from AI output) would map to AVA's AGENTS.md rules system — a 'Save as Rule' button could add to .ava/rules/.

continue_patterns.description: When the model wants to call a tool that requires approval, the toolbar above the input changes to show the pending tool call with Accept/Reject buttons. The tool call shows its displayTitle. First pending call has keyboard shortcuts shown (Cmd+Enter to accept, Cmd+Backspace to reject). Multiple pending tool calls can be queued and shown stacked. Focus returns to editor after last rejection.
continue_patterns.ava_recommendation: AVA's tool approval modal interrupts the flow as a full overlay. Continue's approach of showing it in the persistent toolbar (above the input) is less disruptive. The keyboard shortcuts (Cmd+Enter/Cmd+Backspace) are more learnable than modals. This maps to AVA's approval dock but as inline toolbar content.

continue_patterns.description: After streaming completes, Continue checks if the response ends without terminal punctuation (., ?, !, ```, :) or emoji. If truncated, a 'Continue generation' button appears in the response actions. The 'Continue your response exactly where you left off:' prompt is sent as a user message to trigger continuation.
continue_patterns.ava_recommendation: AVA should detect truncated responses and offer a 'Continue' button. Currently users must manually ask the model to continue. The punctuation-based heuristic is simple and effective. The injected continuation prompt 'Continue your response exactly where you left off:' is a good default.

continue_patterns.description: A two-screen dialog: Input screen asks what behavior to encode as a rule (with template chips like 'Code Style', 'Testing', 'Architecture'), Generation screen streams the generated rule and lets users edit it before saving to .continuerules. Manual write mode skips generation and goes directly to editor.
continue_patterns.ava_recommendation: AVA could add a 'Save as Rule' flow triggered from response actions. The model's response can be condensed into a project rule via a generation step. Maps to adding to .ava/rules/ or AGENTS.md. The template chips ('Code Style', 'Testing') help users frame the rule correctly.

continue_patterns.description: Continue's UnifiedTerminal renders ANSI escape codes with styled spans using the Anser library. Each ANSI sequence produces a styled span with fg/bg colors and decorations (bold, italic, underline, dim, strikethrough, blink). URLs in terminal output are auto-linked. A blinking cursor CSS animation shows at the end of in-progress output. The terminal also supports moving a running process to background.
continue_patterns.ava_recommendation: AVA TUI already renders ANSI via crossterm/ratatui. For the desktop app, terminal output in tool results should use ANSI rendering rather than plain text. The blinking cursor while a command runs is a clear running indicator. The background process feature (move long-running command to background) is directly relevant to AVA's bash tool.

continue_patterns.description: Both Continue and Cline detect ```mermaid code blocks and render them as SVG diagrams inline in the chat. Continue's MermaidBlock component uses the mermaid.js library with the VS Code theme colors. Cline has its own MermaidBlock component. Diagrams render with a copy-source button alongside.
continue_patterns.ava_recommendation: AVA desktop should render Mermaid diagrams inline. Currently ```mermaid blocks show as raw code. For architecture/flow discussions, rendered diagrams are significantly more readable. The mermaid.js library is straightforward to integrate.

continue_patterns.description: Continue's StyledMarkdownPreview detects symbol references (function names, class names) in AI responses and renders them as clickable links that jump to the symbol definition in the editor. Uses a SymbolLink component that calls back to the IDE to navigate to definition.
continue_patterns.ava_recommendation: Only applicable to the desktop app in VS Code extension context. For AVA standalone desktop, could adapt to open in a code viewer. Low priority unless AVA adds code navigation features.

continue_patterns.description: Continue renders LaTeX math expressions using rehype-katex and remark-math plugins. Both inline ($formula$) and block ($$formula$$) math renders as formatted equations.
continue_patterns.ava_recommendation: Relevant only if AVA serves math/science use cases. Low priority for a general coding agent.

codex_patterns.description: Codex TUI implements a per-character shimmer sweep effect using a time-based color interpolation. A band of brighter characters sweeps left-to-right across text at 2s period. Uses truecolor RGB blending when available, falls back to bold/dim/normal for 256-color/16-color terminals. Implemented in shimmer.rs using elapsed-since-process-start timing.

Diff rendering adapts to the terminal's background lightness (dark vs light theme). Dark terminals get muted tints (#213A2B green, #4A221D red). Light terminals get GitHub-style pastel colors (#dafbe1/#ffebe9) with separate gutter backgrounds for line numbers (#aceebb/#ffcecb). Falls back gracefully from truecolor to 256-color to 16-color palettes. Syntax highlighting is preserved within diff hunks using syntect, with hunk-level (not line-level) highlighting to preserve parser state across multi-line strings/comments.

Codex TUI maintains an 'active cell' that coalesces multiple related tool calls (exec/tool group) into a single mutable in-flight display cell that updates in place during streaming. Committed cells are immutable. The transcript overlay appends a cached live tail from the active cell using a composite cache key (revision + time). When the active cell mutates or time-dependent output changes, the cache key changes and the overlay refreshes. This prevents rebuild on every draw tick.

Codex TUI has a /agent command and keyboard shortcuts to switch between active agent threads. Each thread has an optional nickname, role (worker/lead/scout), and closed state (renders dimmed). Thread entries show response previews truncated to 240 characters. Fast-switch shortcuts cycle through threads. The active thread renders normally; other threads show as dimmed background agents.

A separate 'transcript' overlay view accessible via Ctrl+T shows the full conversation in a pager-like read-only view. The live active cell is appended as a cached tail. This lets users read past context while the agent is still working, without disrupting the main chat view.

codex_patterns.feature: Markdown Rendering with Local Link Normalization
codex_patterns.description: Codex TUI's markdown renderer uses pulldown-cmark and treats local file paths differently from web URLs. Local paths show the actual destination as display text (not the markdown label), and absolute paths are shortened relative to cwd or home dir. Heading levels h1-h6 have distinct styles (bold+underline, bold, bold+italic, italic). Code spans are cyan-colored.

The TUI has distinct styles for proposed plans (proposed_plan_style) vs regular user messages (user_message_style), reflecting the dual-mode (Plan/Act) nature of the agent. Plans render with a different visual treatment from regular chat.

gemini_patterns.feature: Ink-Based TUI with Semantic Color System
gemini_patterns.description: Gemini CLI uses Ink (React for terminals) with a centralized semantic-colors module that maps semantic names (text.accent, text.secondary, status.error) to theme colors. Components reference semantic colors not raw ANSI codes. This makes the entire color palette swappable without touching components.

gemini_patterns.feature: Queued Message Display with Up-Arrow Edit
gemini_patterns.description: When messages are queued (mid-stream messaging), Gemini CLI shows up to 3 queued messages below the input with 'Queued (press up to edit):' label. Messages are shown as single-line previews (whitespace collapsed, truncated). If more than 3 queued, shows '... (+N more)'. The up-arrow key lets users edit queued messages before they are sent.

A persistent status line above the input shows active context: '2 open files (ctrl+g to view) | 1 GEMINI.md file | 3 MCP servers | 2 skills | 1 Background process'. Adapts to narrow terminals: in narrow mode, each item is on its own line prefixed with '-'. Only renders items that are non-zero. On narrow terminals, flexDirection changes to column layout. Blocked MCP servers shown separately.

When rendering unified diffs, if hunks are separated by more than MAX_CONTEXT_LINES_WITHOUT_GAP skipped lines, a gap indicator is rendered (a full-width row of '═' characters, terminal-width wide). Small gaps (within threshold) show all context lines without a gap marker. This gives users a clear visual break between non-adjacent code sections without wasting space.

gemini_patterns.feature: ANSI Diff Rendering with Language-Aware Syntax Highlighting
gemini_patterns.description: Gemini's DiffRenderer detects the file extension for new files and passes the language to colorizeCode. New files with known extensions get full syntax highlighting. Unknown extensions get null language (plain). The diff renders with standard unified diff format, supporting both git diff and SVN diff formats. Available terminal height is passed to the renderer to enable height-constrained output.

When lifecycle hooks are active, the status bar switches to show hook status instead of context summary. HookStatusDisplay shows which hooks are currently running. This gives live feedback during hook execution (pre-tool, post-tool, etc.).

A ShellModeIndicator component visually flags when the agent is in 'shell mode' (a mode where bash commands are directly available without tool call overhead). This is distinct from normal agent mode.
A dedicated view (AgentsStatus) lists all active background agents with their status. Gemini CLI has background multi-agent support where sub-agents run concurrently and their results are aggregated.

summary:
Four tools, four distinct approaches to the same core UX problems.

The single most impactful pattern to adopt from Cline is tool grouping: collapsing low-stakes read-only tool calls (file reads, searches, glob) into a single summary row with compact list display. This directly addresses AVA's verbose tool-call display in long exploration tasks.

From Cline's diff view: the inline diff block with live streaming scroll-follow, +N/-N stat chips, and click-to-open-in-editor is the gold standard for showing file edits. The 4px left-border gutter stripe (instead of full row background) is a visual refinement worth adopting.

From Continue's code block toolbar: the Apply/Accept/Reject flow for code blocks gives users explicit control over when changes are applied. This is more deliberate than AVA's current auto-apply. The context-aware Compact button (turns warning-yellow at 80% context) is an elegant passive nudge.

From Continue's thinking display: showing 'Thought for 2.3s (1847 tokens)' after reasoning completes is immediately informative. The pill-shaped collapsible chip is more compact than AVA's current thinking block. Continue also handles redacted thinking blocks gracefully.

The most unique patterns not found in other tools: (1) Cline's FocusChain (live progress bar with current TODO step in the task header), (2) Cline's user message edit + two-tier checkpoint restore (chat vs chat+workspace), (3) Continue's truncation detection with auto-continue, (4) Continue's Generate Rule dialog (extract project rules from AI responses), (5) Codex's per-character shimmer sweep for streaming text, (6) Codex's theme-aware diff palettes for dark vs light terminals, (7) Gemini's gap indicator for non-adjacent diff hunks, (8) Gemini's context summary status line with active context breakdown.
